sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Does EFSC’s Strong Q3 Loan and Deposit Growth Change The Bull Case For Enterprise Financial Services?
feed.summary: Enterprise Financial Services (EFSC) reported solid Q3 results with annualized loan growth of 6% and even higher deposit growth, boosted by a 12-branch acquisition. While analysts' expectations for revenue and EPS remained stable, the market's modest reaction suggests the news is important but not thesis-changing, especially considering ongoing credit quality concerns related to nonperforming assets and commercial real estate. Investors should weigh the reinforced growth narrative against potential risks from credit strains.

feed.title: Gauzy Ltd. Announces Changes to Management and Board of Directors
feed.summary: Gauzy Ltd. announced changes to its management and Board of Directors, including the resignation of CFO Meir Peleg and the appointment of Dan Oshri as Interim CFO. Board members Lilach Payorski and Alexander Babitsky also stepped down. The company also disclosed a proposal for a $50 million equity line of credit facility from an existing shareholder to address liquidity challenges and support operations.

feed.title: Beyond The Numbers: 4 Analysts Discuss GRAIL Stock
feed.summary: Four analysts have provided ratings for GRAIL (NASDAQ: GRAL) in the last quarter, with an average price target of $111.25, reflecting a 23.61% increase from the prior average. The company, focused on early cancer detection, shows positive revenue growth but lags behind peers in its sector, while demonstrating strong net margins and conservative debt management. Analysts' assessments are crucial for investors seeking insights into GRAIL's market standing and future prospects.

feed.title: Form DEF 14A Keysight Technologies, For: Mar 19
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) has filed its definitive proxy statement (Form DEF 14A) for its annual stockholder meeting scheduled for March 19, 2026. The meeting will be held virtually and will cover the election of three directors, ratification of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm, an advisory vote on executive compensation, and a proposal to declassify the Board of Directors. The board also addresses a stockholder proposal for shareholders to call special meetings, recommending a vote against it due to potential inefficiencies and strong existing corporate governance practices.

feed.title: Enterprise Financial reports $54.8M in Q4 net income as deposits surge by $1B
feed.summary: Enterprise Financial reported a Q4 net income of $54.8 million. The St. Louis-based bank experienced a significant increase in deposits, which surged by approximately $1 billion during the quarter. This growth was partly attributed to strategic branch acquisitions that also brought in hundreds of millions in new loans.

feed.title: Daxor Signs Multiple Material Agreements
feed.summary: Daxor (DXR) has entered into two significant agreements to support a registered direct equity offering. The company signed a Securities Purchase Agreement with investors to sell 765,958 common shares at $11.75 per share, resulting in approximately $8.14 million in net proceeds. Additionally, Daxor signed a Placement Agency Agreement with Lake Street Capital Markets, which facilitated the transaction that closed on January 26, 2026, with proceeds earmarked for general corporate and growth needs.

feed.title: Lam Research Earnings: What To Look For From LRCX
feed.summary: Lam Research is set to report its latest earnings this Wednesday after market close, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 20% year over year to $5.25 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.17 per share. The company has a history of consistently beating revenue estimates. Investor sentiment in the semiconductor segment has been positive, with Lam Research's stock outperforming peers recently.

feed.title: King Sarah H, SVP at Darden Restaurants, sells $951k in stock
feed.summary: Sarah H. King, SVP, Chief People Officer at Darden Restaurants Inc, sold 4,605 shares of common stock on January 22, 2026, totaling $951,636. Following these transactions, King directly owns 670.7710 shares. Darden Restaurants has maintained dividend payments for 32 consecutive years and analysts have recently provided varied but generally positive outlooks on the company.

feed.title: IBM (IBM) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: IBM is scheduled to report earnings tomorrow, with expectations for 9.5% year-on-year revenue growth to $19.21 billion and adjusted earnings of $4.29 per share. Analysts have largely maintained their estimates, suggesting a consistent performance is anticipated. Despite some past revenue misses, peer performance in the IT services sector and IBM's current financial position (with an average analyst price target above its current share price) provide context for the upcoming report.

feed.title: Waste Management (WM) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) is set to report its earnings tomorrow after market close, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 8.5% year-on-year to $6.39 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.95 per share. The company missed revenue expectations last quarter and has done so four times in the last two years. Despite this, positive investor sentiment in the environmental and facilities services segment has seen share prices rise, with WM's stock up 3.5% over the last month, approaching an average analyst price target of $248.56.

feed.title: Carnival Corporation named one of the World’s most admired companies for second consecutive year
feed.summary: Carnival Corporation has been recognized for the second consecutive year on Fortune’s World’s Most Admired Companies list. The world's largest cruise company was included based on its strong industry leadership, financial performance, and global competitiveness, making it one of only eight companies in the Hotels, Casinos and Resorts category. CEO Josh Weinstein credited the company's 160,000 global team members for this achievement.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) Q4 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday. Analysts expect revenue to decrease by 4.7% year-on-year to $3.99 billion and adjusted earnings per share to be $1.13. The company has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times recently, while the transportation and logistics segment has shown positive investor sentiment.

feed.title: United Rentals (URI) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) is set to report its earnings this Wednesday. The company beat revenue expectations last quarter but missed EPS estimates. Analysts predict a 3.4% year-on-year revenue growth to $4.24 billion and adjusted earnings of $11.80 per share.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: AT&T (T) Reports Q4 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: AT&T (NYSE: T) is set to announce its Q4 earnings tomorrow, with analysts expecting revenues to grow 1.5% year-on-year to $32.79 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.47 per share. The company met revenue expectations last quarter but missed Mobility revenue estimates, and its stock is down 4.9% over the last month. Investors will be watching keenly after mixed results from peers in the consumer discretionary segment.

feed.title: Textron Earnings: What To Look For From TXT
feed.summary: Textron (TXT) is scheduled to report earnings this Wednesday before market open, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 12.9% year-on-year to $4.08 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.70 per share. The company has a history of missing Wall Street’s revenue estimates and investors are watching closely, especially given recent mixed performance from peers in the aerospace and defense sector. Textron's stock is up 7.4% recently and analysts have an average price target of $98.41.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: Corning (GLW) Reports Q4 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: Corning (GLW) is set to report its Q4 earnings tomorrow, with analysts expecting a 12.8% year-on-year revenue growth to $4.37 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.71 per share. The company has a strong record of exceeding revenue estimates, beating them every quarter over the last two years. Positive sentiment in the electrical equipment sector, where Corning operates, suggests a favorable environment as the company approaches its earnings release.

feed.title: Harlem’s Dollar Tree fined $560K for selling lead-tainted baby food after recall
feed.summary: New York Attorney General Letitia James announced that Dollar Tree will pay a $559,250 penalty for selling lead-contaminated WanaBana cinnamon applesauce pouches even after a nationwide recall in October 2023. The investigation revealed that Dollar Tree delayed activating sales prevention measures, allowing hundreds of units to be sold for days and even weeks post-recall. The settlement funds will support lead poisoning prevention programs and food access in New York, and Dollar Tree must also overhaul its recall procedures.

feed.title: What To Expect From Danaher’s (DHR) Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Danaher (DHR) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 4.2% year-on-year to $6.81 billion and adjusted earnings of $2.19 per share. The company has a history of exceeding revenue estimates and is heading into earnings with positive investor sentiment in the life sciences tools & services sector. Danaher's stock is up 2.8% in the last month, with an average analyst price target of $263.05.

feed.title: MSCI (MSCI) Q4 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: MSCI (NYSE:MSCI), an investment analytics provider, is set to report its Q4 earnings before market open this Wednesday. Analysts anticipate an 11% year-on-year revenue growth to $824.9 million and adjusted earnings of $4.58 per share. While MSCI met revenue expectations last quarter, it has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates three times in the past two years.

feed.title: Ford and General Motors in talks with First Brands over rescue financing, FT reports
feed.summary: Ford and General Motors are in negotiations with bankrupt car parts supplier First Brands Group over a potential financing package. The financing aims to keep First Brands operating during its Chapter 11 proceedings, as reported by the Financial Times. First Brands has begun winding down parts of its North American operations while seeking buyers for remaining assets.

feed.title: The Problem Lockheed Martin Ran Into When Delivering The F-35 Last Year
feed.summary: In 2025, Lockheed Martin delivered a record 191 F-35 Joint Strike Fighters, a figure that on the surface suggested a positive turning point for the program. However, this surge in deliveries was primarily due to the clearing of a backlog caused by delays in certifying the Technology Refresh 3 (TR-3) upgrade. The TR-3 upgrade, vital for the F-35's modernization and future capabilities, experienced significant software integration and testing challenges, leading to a pause in deliveries throughout much of 2023 and 2024, despite continued production.

feed.title: Salling Group joins WPP Media's Open Intelligence for Commerce 01/27/2026
feed.summary: Salling Group, Denmark's largest retailer, has partnered with WPP Media to integrate its first-party data with WPP’s Open Intelligence for Commerce platform. This collaboration will connect Salling's weekly customer data from its retail chains across six European countries with WPP's platform to generate commerce insights and enable custom targeting for WPP clients. Salling's own retail media network is also expected to benefit from this partnership, enhancing data-driven insights and media activation while maintaining privacy standards.

feed.title: Ellington Financial launches 8.7 million share offering to redeem preferred stock
feed.summary: Ellington Financial Inc. has announced a public offering of 8.775 million shares of common stock, with an option for underwriters to purchase additional shares. The company plans to use the net proceeds to redeem its Series A Preferred Stock and for general corporate purposes, including potential asset acquisitions. This follows a strong Q3 2025 earnings performance, exceeding analyst forecasts, and an expansion of its at-the-market stock offering.

feed.title: Micron Breaks Ground on Advanced Wafer Fabrication Facility in Singapore
feed.summary: Micron Technology has broken ground on an advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore, representing a planned investment of approximately US $24 billion over 10 years. The facility, which will ultimately provide 700,000 square feet of cleanroom space and begin wafer output in late 2028, aims to meet growing market demand for NAND technology driven by AI. This expansion is expected to create around 1,600 new jobs, complementing another 1,400 jobs from an HBM advanced packaging facility, further solidifying Singapore's role in the global semiconductor supply chain.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Starbucks: "It Can't Keep Climbing Unless the Numbers Are Insanely Strong"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX), deeming the stock "wildly overbought" ahead of its earnings report and an investor day. He believes that for the stock to continue climbing, its numbers need to be exceptionally strong, as its recent rally may not be justifiable otherwise. Cramer likes Starbucks for the long term but doubts the company can tell a story sufficient to support its current valuation.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Union Pacific’s Merger
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE: UNP) and its potential merger with Norfolk Southern, calling transports the "hottest group" in the market. The article notes that while Union Pacific is doing well, the publication believes certain AI stocks offer greater promise for higher returns in a shorter timeframe. Union Pacific operates in the railroad industry, transporting various goods.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Union Pacific's Merger
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE: UNP) as a focus stock, noting that transports, including rails, are the "hottest group" in the market. He specifically mentioned Union Pacific's efforts to merge with Norfolk Southern, calling it a "great situation." The article also suggests that while UNP has investment potential, some AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: Fatburger and Johnny Rockets owner seeks Chapter 11 reset
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc. announced it has filed voluntary Chapter 11 petitions in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas to deleverage its balance sheet and maximize stakeholder value. The company, which owns 18 restaurant brands including Fatburger and Johnny Rockets, expects all of its over 2,200 locations to continue operating as usual. Trading of FAT Brands' securities on NASDAQ is expected to continue with a "Q" suffix during the Chapter 11 process.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Caterpillar: “In the old days, CAT usually went down on earnings, that’s over”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Caterpillar (CAT) earnings, noting a shift in investor reaction. Traditionally, Caterpillar's stock would drop after earnings reports, but Cramer suggests this pattern has changed. His statement indicates improved market sentiment or fundamental performance for the company.

feed.title: Fat Brands Files Voluntary Chapter 11 Petitions
feed.summary: FAT Brands has filed for voluntary Chapter 11 petitions. The news was reported by Reuters, indicating a development in the financial status of the company. This action may impact the company's operations and stock performance.

feed.title: Johnson And Johnson Data Updates Reframe Oncology And Neuropsychiatry Outlook
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has announced positive Phase 3 results for TECVAYLI in multiple myeloma and new CAPLYTA data showing improved remission rates in major depressive disorder. These clinical updates provide context for the company's strong stock performance and highlight potential revenue drivers in oncology and neuropsychiatry. Investors will be watching for regulatory decisions, label expansions, and real-world adoption to assess the impact on business outcomes.

feed.title: FAT Brands Inc. Files Voluntary Chapter 11 Petitions to Bolster Capital Structure
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc. has filed voluntary Chapter 11 petitions in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas. The company aims to use these proceedings to deleverage its balance sheet, maximize stakeholder value, and support the continued growth of its 18 restaurant brands, which include Fatburger and Johnny Rockets. Operations for these brands are expected to continue as usual during the Chapter 11 process.

feed.title: Constellation Energy Weighs AI Power Growth Against Nuclear Pricing Caps
feed.summary: Constellation Energy has recently acquired Calpine Corporation and secured long-term clean energy contracts with major tech clients like Microsoft and Meta Platforms, positioning itself to benefit from rising AI and cloud computing demand. However, the company faces uncertainty from potential regulatory caps on power pricing, which could impact the profitability of its nuclear fleet. This mix of expansion and policy pressure has contributed to a significant decline in its share price despite strong three-year returns.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Michael Rosenbaum Sells Shares of Guidewire Softwa
feed.summary: Michael Rosenbaum, CEO of Guidewire Software Inc (GWRE), sold 1,200 shares of the company on January 26, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 234,343 shares. Over the past year, Rosenbaum has sold 119,394 shares with no purchases, and the company has seen 94 insider sells versus 0 buys.

feed.title: Micron Invests $24 Billion in Singapore to Boost Chip Output
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. plans to invest an additional $24 billion in Singapore over the next decade to expand its chip manufacturing capabilities. This investment aims to meet the growing demand for memory chips, particularly NAND, driven by the expansion of AI infrastructure. The Boise, Idaho-based company will use the funding to construct a new facility for NAND production.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Corning: “I Think You Have to Just Hope It Comes Down So You Can Buy Some”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recently discussed Corning Incorporated (NYSE: GLW), highlighting its strong prospects, particularly in replacing copper with fiber in data centers. He expressed bullish sentiment for the stock, calling it a potential top performer for 2026 and suggesting investors hope for a dip to buy shares. The article also mentions that Insider Monkey believes some AI stocks hold greater promise than GLW for higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says the Sell-Off After Almost Every American Express Quarter Is "Usually a Perfect Buying Opportunity"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends buying American Express (AXP) stock on dips after earnings reports, noting a consistent pattern of sell-offs despite good quarters, which he views as a "perfect buying opportunity." He maintains a bullish stance on the company, highlighting its strength in the travel-related experiential economy. The article also suggests that while AXP is a potential investment, certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less downside risk.

feed.title: Starbucks stock price slips after hours as Wall Street lines up earnings and investor day (SBUX)
feed.summary: Starbucks shares slipped 1.3% after hours, closing at $96.33, as investors await the company's fiscal first-quarter earnings call on January 28 and an investor day on January 29. Analysts have mixed views, with UBS forecasting revenue growth but Mizuho and Jefferies expressing caution regarding operating margins and potential downside risks, respectively. The upcoming events are crucial for Starbucks to demonstrate momentum in its "Back to Starbucks" turnaround plan.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on General Motors: "CEO Mary Barra Never Gets the Respect She Deserves for What She's Delivered Through the Years"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer praised General Motors CEO Mary Barra, noting her consistent strong performance and the company's significant share buyback activities, which have reduced its share count by over 40% since 2015. He highlighted GM's stock performance, which is up over 50% year-to-date, trading at its highest level since 2010, and suggested that post-earnings dips could be a buying opportunity. Cramer also mentioned that despite its recent gains, the stock remains undervalued, trading at less than seven times next year's estimates.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Expects Western Digital's Quarter to Be "Very Positive"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer predicted a "very positive" quarter for Western Digital (WDC), highlighting its position as a key storage play alongside Sandisk. He emphasized the market's growing appreciation for data center companies and noted Western Digital's significant stock performance throughout 2025, despite historically being undervalued. Cramer believes the market is finally recognizing the company's strong technology.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Traders Don't Understand International Business Machines' Value
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted International Business Machines (IBM) as a strong performer, predicting its stock will quickly recover from any initial market disappointment following its earnings report, similar to the previous quarter. He praised IBM's transformation into a "juggernaut" with robust hybrid cloud, AI, and consulting businesses, led by an exceptional CEO, Arvind Krishna. Cramer also noted positive technical indicators like a bullish crossover and strong relative strength, suggesting the stock is poised for further gains.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses How the Honeywell Breakup Might Impact the Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Honeywell's upcoming breakup, suggesting that while the plans to list its quantum business, Quantinuum, have driven the stock up, the subsequent split into multiple entities (automation, aerospace) could cause it to take a hit. He anticipates that investors may not want all the new separate parts, leading to a potential decline in the stock's value, possibly after its upcoming earnings report.

feed.title: Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol Receives $31 Million Compensation Package in 2025
feed.summary: Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol's total compensation for 2025 dropped to $31 million, a significant decrease from $96 million in 2024, primarily due to the company's 7.7% stock decline. Niccol, who joined in September 2024, is implementing a "Back to Starbucks" strategy which has started to show positive results with recent comparable sales growth. The company is set to release Q4 earnings and financial targets soon, which will offer further insight into his strategy and outlook.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Chevron: "It's Been My Favorite for a Very Long Time Because It's So Darn Consistent"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Chevron (NYSE: CVX) as a consistent favorite due to its significant buyback program, 4% dividend yield, and potential opportunities in Venezuela. He noted Chevron's long-standing interest in Venezuelan oil, suggesting a possible increase in production beyond the current 100,000 barrels per day. Despite the complexities of Venezuela's oil industry and fluctuating oil prices, Cramer views Chevron's Venezuelan involvement as a potential "kicker" for the company, though he also acknowledged that any increase would still be a small portion of the oil giant's overall production.

feed.title: Micron’s $24B Singapore chip fab to add about 3,000 jobs
feed.summary: Micron Technology has broken ground on a new advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore, representing a planned investment of approximately US $24 billion over 10 years. This expansion will add 700,000 square feet of cleanroom space and is expected to create 1,600 new jobs, contributing to a total of 3,000 new jobs in Singapore when combined with the previously announced HBM advanced packaging facility. The new fab aims to address growing market demand for NAND technology, with wafer output slated to begin in the second half of 2028.

feed.title: Century Aluminum Partners With EGA to Build US Aluminum Plant
feed.summary: Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) and Century Aluminum Co. are partnering to construct the first new U.S. aluminum plant since 1980. The facility, to be located in Oklahoma, is projected to produce 750,000 metric tons of aluminum annually. EGA will hold a 60% stake in the joint venture, with Century Aluminum owning the remaining share.

feed.title: Microsoft wins approval for 15 more data centers in Mt. Pleasant, Wisconsin
feed.summary: Microsoft has received approval from local officials in Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin, to construct 15 additional data centers. These new facilities will expand Microsoft's existing presence and aims to boost its computing power and artificial intelligence capabilities. The development is expected to make Microsoft the largest employer in the area, with a taxable value estimated at over $13 billion.

feed.title: AbbVie stock price closes higher; what to watch next for ABBV ahead of Fed week and earnings
feed.summary: AbbVie shares closed 0.7% higher on Monday at $220.77, as investors anticipate the Federal Reserve's decision and upcoming mega-cap earnings reports. The company is set to release its full-year and Q4 2025 results on February 4th, with focus on the performance of new immunology treatments Skyrizi and Rinvoq to offset the decline of Humira. Analysts are also watching AbbVie's pipeline developments, including their obesity drug strategy and a recent cancer drug licensing agreement with RemeGen, while acknowledging risks like Humira's decline, pipeline setbacks, and potential pricing pressures.

feed.title: Chevron stock price ticks up after Winter Storm Fern disrupts U.S. output — what to watch next
feed.summary: Chevron's stock price rose by 0.4% following Winter Storm Fern, which disrupted U.S. crude output by up to 2 million barrels per day and caused U.S. natural gas prices to surge. While the storm temporarily boosted energy stocks, uncertainties remain regarding future supply policy, potential legal issues in Kazakhstan, and changing regulations in Venezuela. Investors are now looking to Chevron's upcoming quarterly earnings release on January 30th for updates on storm impact and the 2026 outlook.

feed.title: Domino's follows McDonald's menu plan, adds bigger deals
feed.summary: Domino's Pizza has adopted a value-focused strategy, similar to McDonald's, to attract cost-conscious consumers amidst rising food prices. Domino's CEO Russell Weiner highlighted the success of their "Best Deal Ever" promotion, which offers any pizza with any toppings for $9.99, contributing to traffic growth across all income groups. Both companies acknowledge the challenges faced by lower-income customers and are actively implementing deals to maintain customer loyalty and sales.
